evidences.title: Python Script for Extracting K-Line Candles Data from Binance
evidences.content: The Python Script The script uses Python's requests library to interact with the Binance API. It fetches data within a specified date range and interval (or timeframe) for a given trading pair, such as BTC/USDT. The script is capable of handling multiple timeframes and neatly organizes the fetched data into CSV files for each timeframe. [...] import requests import datetime import time import csv # Functions for working with time def get_milliseconds(date_str): epoch = datetime.datetime.utcfromtimestamp(0) return int((datetime.datetime.strptime(date_str, "%d %b %Y") - epoch).total_seconds() 1000.0) # Function for getting data from Binance def get_binance_data(symbol, start_str, end_str, interval): url = " start_time = get_milliseconds(start_str) end_time = get_milliseconds(end_str) limit = 1000 all_data = [] while start_time < end_time: params = { 'symbol': symbol, 'interval': interval, 'limit': limit, 'startTime': start_time, 'endTime': end_time } response = requests.get(url, params=params) if response.status_code == 200: data = response.json() if not data: break all_data.extend(data) start_time = data[-1] + 1 else: break [...] all_data.extend(data) start_time = data[-1] + 1 el...

evidences.title: BITCOIN Historical Datasets 2018-2026 Binance API - Kaggle
evidences.content: Open time: The timestamp for when the interval began. Open: The price of Bitcoin at the beginning of the interval. High: The highest price during the interval. Low: The lowest price during the interval. Close: The price of Bitcoin at the end of the interval. Volume: The trading volume during the interval. Close time: The timestamp for when the interval closed. Quote asset volume: The total quote asset volume traded during the interval. Number of trades: The number of trades executed within the interval. Taker buy base asset volume: The volume of the base asset bought by takers. Taker buy quote asset volume: The volume of the quote asset spent by takers. Ignore: A placeholder column from Binance API, not used in analysis. ## Data Sources [...] ## Dataset Overview This dataset contains historical price data for Bitcoin (BTC/USDT) from January 1, 2018, to the present. The data is sourced using the Binance API, providing granular candlestick data in four timeframes: 15-minute (15M) 1-hour (1H) 4-hour (4H) 1-day (1D) This dataset includes the following fields for each timeframe: [...] 2018-02-18 11039.55 11274.0 10080.0 10383.43 61137.380728 2018-02-18 23:59:59.999 649423311.3100228...

evidences.title: Save Historical data from Binance
evidences.content: ``` frombinance.client import Client # fetch 1 minute klines for the last day up until now klines = get_historical_klines("BNBBTC", Client. KLINE_INTERVAL_1MINUTE, "1 day ago UTC") # fetch 30 minute klines for the last month of 2017 klines = get_historical_klines("ETHBTC", Client. KLINE_INTERVAL_30MINUTE,"1 Dec, 2017","1 Jan, 2018") # fetch weekly klines since it listed klines = get_historical_klines("NEOBTC", KLINE_INTERVAL_1WEEK,"1 Jan, 2017") ``` The full code can be found in the examples folder of the python-binance package. ### Save to file Once we have fetched the list of klines, it makes sense to save them to a file for later use. [...] First install the `dateparser` package ``` install ``` Now we can create a function to take a readable string into milliseconds. With this function we can convert all sorts of handy date formats to milliseconds like the examples below. ``` print(date_to_milliseconds("January 01, 2018")) print(date_to_milliseconds("11 hours ago UTC")) print(date_to_milliseconds("now UTC")) ``` ### Binance Kline endpoint Now we have that out of the way we can start to work with the Binance API. For our purposes we are interested in the get\_klines endpoint t...

evidences.title: How do I get Binance API prices in Python at a precise time?
evidences.content: The following is how I get historical data, however the date format does not allow me to specify the minute or second of the data; only the day, which is not precise enough. `start_date_str=start_date.strftime("%d %b, %Y") data1=client.get_historical_klines(pair, Client.KLINE_INTERVAL_1MINUTE, start_date_str)` I'm looking to get price data for a particular hour, minute and second using the python binance api provided by Sam McHardy (link below). If anyone knows whether this is possible, or if there are any alternatives, I would really appreciate it. Cam K's user avatar ## 2 Answers 2 [...] Cam K's user avatar ## 2 Answers 2 KLINE\_INTERVAL\_1MINUTE is the smallest time resolution for get\_historical\_klines provided by Binance. If you need smaller time intervals you can use get\_aggregate\_trades which allows startTime and endTime in milliseconds. This request return the list of trades, so one has to calculate average price abd total volume for desired time period. Yuri Ginsburg's user avatar ## Comments Add a comment current syntax is : (symbol)@kline\_(interval) for example `BTCUSDT@kline_1m (one minute)` for autobahn complete json : `def onOpen(self): subscribe_message = { "m...
